Pain.definition: a symptom of some physical hurt or disorder

Hurt.definition: any physical damage to the body caused by violence or accident or fracture etc.

Injury.definition: any physical damage to the body caused by violence or accident or fracture etc.

Tingle.definition: an almost pleasurable sensation of fright

Itch.definition: an irritating cutaneous sensation that produces a desire to scratch

Bite.definition: the act of gripping or chewing off with the teeth and jaws

Cramp.definition: a painful and involuntary muscular contraction

Swollen.definition: increase in size, magnitude, number, or intensity

Sprain.definition: a painful injury to a joint caused by a sudden wrenching of its ligaments

Trip.definition: a journey for some purpose (usually including the return)

Fracture.definition: breaking of hard tissue such as bone

Accident.definition: an unfortunate mishap; especially one causing damage or injury

Burn.definition: pain that feels hot as if it were on fire

Sting.definition: a kind of pain; something as sudden and painful as being stung

Ache.definition: a dull persistent (usually moderately intense) pain

Headache.definition: something or someone that causes anxiety; a source of unhappiness
pain in the head caused by dilation of cerebral arteries or muscle contractions or a reaction to drugs

Wound.definition: an injury to living tissue (especially an injury involving a cut or break in the skin)

Fever.definition: a rise in the temperature of the body; frequently a symptom of infection
